human_verification.notes: Reviewed Issue #1382 requirements for government-and-public-sector public data reporting, source authority quality, research coverage, scenario completeness, due-process boundaries, and validated specification-based packaging consistency.

review_notes: All required abilities across shared, analytics-reporting-and-disclosure, and government-and-public-sector sets are declared and validated.

Scenario coverage includes normal reporting package assembly, metric ambiguity, stale or conflicting source data, unsupported claims, incomplete submissions, rights-sensitive exceptions, missing audit trails, adjudication and enforcement refusal, statutory interpretation escalation, adjacent-lane routing, tenant adaptation, and conflicting policy handling.
Package remains specification-based and documents a separate promotion path for any future retained-implementation runtime.

specialist_owned_evidence: Outputs are bounded to metric definitions, source-tied public reports, submission summaries, caveats, reconciliation notes, disclosure-risk notes, and escalation packages.
Source set includes OMB, eCFR, NARA, DOJ, FAR, GAO, and U.S. Code references with explicit authority rationale, refresh interval, decay policy, and review date in the manifest.

Boundary logic prevents unsupported claims, public records release decisions, procurement approvals, audit findings, statutory interpretation, adjudication, enforcement, and final publication signoff.
notes: No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains; non-blocking ambiguity is recorded as tenant and jurisdiction prerequisites for exact public records, privacy, eligibility, metric, and signoff variants.
Runtime remains `specification-based`; retained-implementation promotion requires separate governance approval, usage evidence, implementation PR, and non-regression validation.
